@@ -0,0 +1,156 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: ad80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Directional navigation from a focused card: all four directions, one ticket'
+---
+## What
+
+When a card is focused, all four direction keys (hjkl in vim, ArrowKeys in cua, Ctrl+p/n/b/f in emacs) must navigate to the user-visible expected target. **One ticket for all four directions.** Future bugs in card-nav add test cases to this card; they do not open new per-direction cards.
+
+This card supersedes:
+- `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ` (cross-column right) — currently `doing`. Move to done after this card lands.
+- `01KQ7RR4MJJPVTTB7GS6RKRH9E` (vertical up/down on cards) — `todo`. Move to done.
+
+The unified-policy card `01KQ7S6WHK9RCCG2R4FN474EFD` covers nav from non-card entries (perspective bar, navbar, inspector). This card focuses on the card case explicitly.
+
+### The user-visible expectations
+
+For a board with three columns (TODO, DOING, DONE), each with three cards (T1, T2, T3 per column):
+
+**Down from a card:**
+- Down from T1A → T2A (next card below in same column).
+- Down from T2A → T3A.
+- Down from T3A (last card in column) → no-op (no card below within the column; do not bounce to another column at a different row).
+
+**Up from a card:**
+- Up from T2A → T1A.
+- Up from T1A (topmost card) → `column:TODO.name` (the column header leaf, geographically above the card).
+- Up from `column:TODO.name` → escalates further (handled by unified-policy card; this card asserts only the card-side step lands on the header).
+
+**Right from a card:**
+- Right from T1A → a card in column DOING (currently expected: T1B, the same-row card in the next column; if DOING has fewer cards or different layout, the kernel picks the geographically closest).
+- Right from T1C (rightmost column) → no-op (no column to the right).
+
+**Left from a card:**
+- Left from T1B → a card in column TODO.
+- Left from T1A (leftmost column) → no-op (no column to the left within the board; the unified-policy card handles drill-out to navbar via Up).
+
+### Why this card is narrower than the unified-policy card
+
+The unified-policy card (`01KQ7S6WHK9RCCG2R4FN474EFD`) is the architectural fix: rewrite `BeamNavStrategy::next` as one cascade replacing the leaf/zone split. It covers every focused entry type and every layer.
+
+This card is the user-visible spec specifically for the card case. It can be implemented:
+- (a) as a thin layer over the unified-policy card (each direction's behavior comes for free once the cascade lands), or
+- (b) as a standalone fix using the existing kernel rules (rule 1 + rule 2 + registry-shape audit) without touching the kernel.
+
+The implementer chooses. Either way, the user-visible acceptance is the same: from a focused card, all four directions produce the expected target.
+
+### Files in scope
+
+**If implemented via the unified-policy card (recommended):**
+- This card adds the test; the kernel changes happen under `01KQ7S6WHK9RCCG2R4FN474EFD`.
+
+**If implemented standalone:**
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — verify column zone wraps cards directly.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.tsx` — verify card body is a `<FocusScope>` leaf with `parent_zone === <column zone key>`.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` — verify the board chrome `ui:board` zone is the parent of column zones.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` — verify Field zones don't intercept clicks meant for the card body.
+
+**Test file (regardless of implementation path):**
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/card-directional-nav.spatial.test.tsx` (new, supersedes `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` and any vertical-card-nav file from `01KQ7RR4MJJPVTTB7GS6RKRH9E`).
+
+### What this card does NOT do
+
+- Does not test directional nav from non-card focused entries — that's the unified-policy card's job (Trajectories A, C, D).
+- Does not test click → focus on cards — that's the click regression suite (`01KQ7SPWRGG4AHTQ3RBNMPMG97`).
+- Does not test drill-in or drill-out semantics — separate concern.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/card-directional-nav.spatial.test.tsx` exists and contains four named tests (one per direction) plus the "no-op at edges" cases.
+- [ ] Each named test mounts the production board view in standard providers, drives focus to a card via `fireFocusChanged`, presses the appropriate key, and asserts the resulting `data-focused="true"` element is the user-visible expected target. Asserts on outcome, not kernel mechanism.
+- [ ] All four directions pass for all three keymaps (vim, cua, emacs).
+- [ ] No-op cases pass: Down from the bottom card in a column, Up from the column header (handed off to the layer above this card's scope), Right from the rightmost column, Left from the leftmost column.
+- [ ] The previous tactical test files are either deleted or merged into the new file. There is **one** test file for directional nav from cards, not three.
+- [ ] `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ` and `01KQ7RR4MJJPVTTB7GS6RKRH9E` are moved to `done` with a "subsumed by this ULID" note in their description. Their existing test files migrate into this card's test file.
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` is green.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` is green.
+
+## Tests
+
+### Browser test (mandatory)
+
+Run under Vitest browser mode (`vitest-browser-react` + Playwright Chromium). Uses the JS shadow-registry harness; whichever extraction lands first (`01KQ7PXYP62VQ18K9XYS4Y42GA` or this card) is fine.
+
+#### Test file
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/card-directional-nav.spatial.test.tsx`
+
+#### Setup
+
+- Mock `@tauri-apps/api/core` and `@tauri-apps/api/event` per the canonical `vi.hoisted` pattern.
+- Render the board view inside the standard provider stack — `<UIStateProvider><PerspectivesProvider><ViewsProvider><BoardDataProvider><SpatialFocusProvider><FocusLayer name="window"><BoardView/></FocusLayer></SpatialFocusProvider>…</UIStateProvider>`.
+- Fixture: 3 columns × 3 cards each, unique titles.
+
+#### Required test cases
+
+**Group: Down**
+1. `down_from_t1a_lands_on_t2a` — `task:T1A` → `task:T2A`.
+2. `down_from_t2a_lands_on_t3a` — `task:T2A` → `task:T3A`.
+3. `down_from_t3a_no_op` — `task:T3A` → no `focus-changed`, `data-focused` stays.
+
+**Group: Up**
+4. `up_from_t2a_lands_on_t1a` — `task:T2A` → `task:T1A`.
+5. `up_from_t1a_lands_on_column_header` — `task:T1A` → `column:TODO.name` (or whatever the header leaf moniker is — capture from registration calls).
+
+**Group: Right**
+6. `right_from_t1a_lands_on_a_card_in_doing` — `task:T1A` → `task:T1B` (or geographically nearest card in column DOING).
+7. `right_from_t1c_no_op` — `task:T1C` (rightmost column) → no `focus-changed`.
+
+**Group: Left**
+8. `left_from_t1b_lands_on_a_card_in_todo` — `task:T1B` → `task:T1A`.
+9. `left_from_t1a_no_op` — `task:T1A` (leftmost) → no `focus-changed`.
+
+**Group: keymap parity**
+10. `cua_arrow_keys_match_vim_hjkl` — same fixture, same focused card, ArrowDown produces the same target as `j`. Repeat for all four directions.
+11. `emacs_ctrl_keybindings_match` — Ctrl+n / Ctrl+p / Ctrl+b / Ctrl+f produce the same targets as the cua/vim equivalents.
+
+Each test asserts on:
+- The `data-focused="true"` element after `fireFocusChanged`.
+- The `[data-testid="focus-indicator"]` mounted under the new focused element.
+- The `mockInvoke` call list contains exactly one `spatial_navigate` call with the correct direction.
+
+### Backend regression test
+
+- [ ] Add `card_directional_nav_all_four_directions` to `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs`. Builds the realistic board shape (3 columns × 3 cards + column.name leaves) and asserts:
+  - `nav("task:T1A", Down) == Some("task:T2A")`
+  - `nav("task:T1A", Up) == Some("column:TODO.name")`
+  - `nav("task:T1A", Right) == Some(card-in-DOING)` (or `column:DOING` if the unified-policy escalation is the path; pin whichever the kernel does)
+  - `nav("task:T1A", Left) == None`
+  - Mirrors for the other corner cases.
+
+This Rust test is the source of truth for the algorithm. The browser test verifies wiring.
+
+### How to run
+
+```
+cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- card-directional-nav
+cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus
+```
+
+Both must pass headless.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd`. Order:
+  1. Write the new test file with all 11 cases as `it.todo` placeholders.
+  2. Implement Down cases (1-3) first. Confirm pass against current production code (the user said up/down works on cards in a column now, so 1-3 should be green already).
+  3. Implement Up cases (4-5). Confirm.
+  4. Implement Right cases (6-7). The user just reported "left right not working" → these are the failing cases. Diagnose the failure mode, fix, confirm pass.
+  5. Implement Left cases (8-9). Mirror.
+  6. Implement keymap parity (10-11).
+  7. Add the Rust regression test.
+  8. Move `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ` and `01KQ7RR4MJJPVTTB7GS6RKRH9E` to done with the supersession note.
+- Future bugs in card-directional-nav add a new `it` to this file. Do not open new per-direction cards. Period.

+## Implementation Notes
+
+The realistic-app fixture surfaced a real kernel bug: rule 2 (cross-zone leaf fallback) was picking up `ui:navbar.search` when the user pressed `right` from `task:T1C` (rightmost column). The leaf was geometrically to the right of T1C but vertically far above the card grid (in the navbar strip), so it was out-of-beam — yet the kernel's existing scoring picked it up because no in-beam candidate existed.
+
+**Fix:** `pick_best_candidate` in `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs` now applies the in-beam test as a **hard filter** for cardinal directions (up/down/left/right), not the previous soft tier preference. Out-of-beam candidates are dropped before scoring runs. This matches the user's mental model of cardinal navigation as "move within the visually-aligned strip" and fixes the user-visible bug without disturbing any other test in the suite — every existing test that asserts a specific cardinal-direction target already places that target in-beam, so the assertions hold unchanged.
+
+The JS shadow navigator inside `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` was updated to mirror the new hard-filter rule so the wiring tests stay consistent with the Rust kernel.
